name: Policy & procedure writing
current_process: Care homes are required to operate a policy-driven approach to governance, with comprehensive policy sets typically numbering 140–300+ documents covering safeguarding, medication management, infection control, health and safety, HR, complaints, data protection, and more. Each policy must be dated, version-controlled, assigned an owner, and reviewed at least annually — or sooner when legislation changes, incidents occur, or CQC issues new guidance. The typical process involves the registered manager or a designated policy owner reviewing each document against current regulations and best practices, making updates in a Word document, getting sign-off from the provider's board or responsible individual, and distributing the updated version to staff. Many providers subscribe to policy library services (QCS, W&P Training, PolicyPros) that supply template policies requiring local customisation. However, customising a generic template to reflect the home's specific practices, client group, and local context still requires significant manual effort. Version control is frequently poor — outdated policies remain in circulation, staff read superseded versions, and the review schedule slips when operational pressures take priority. Staff acknowledgement that they have read and understood updated policies is often tracked via paper sign-off sheets.
time_spent: Estimated 4–8 hours per month on policy review and updating for a typical care home — more during periods of regulatory change. Writing a new policy from scratch takes 3–6 hours including research, drafting, consultation, and formatting. Annual review of the full policy suite (200+ documents) represents a rolling workload of 100–200+ hours per year. Many registered managers report that policy reviews are one of the first tasks to slip when they are under operational pressure, creating compliance risk.
ai_solution: AI can dramatically accelerate policy writing, review, and version management: (1) AI-powered policy drafting — given a topic and the relevant regulations, AI generates a compliant first draft tailored to the care home's size, client group, and service type, reducing drafting time from hours to minutes. (2) Automated regulatory change detection — AI monitors CQC guidance updates, legislative changes, and NICE guideline revisions, and flags which existing policies need updating. (3) Intelligent review assistance — AI compares existing policies against current regulations and best practice, highlighting outdated references, missing requirements, and inconsistencies. (4) Automated version control with audit trails — timestamped versions, tracked changes, and automatic archiving of superseded documents. (5) Staff acknowledgement tracking — digital read-and-confirm workflows with automated reminders and compliance dashboards showing which staff have read which policies. (6) Plain-English readability checks to ensure policies are accessible to all staff, including those with English as a second language. ReflowAI offers AI-powered compliance documentation for UK care homes.
buyer_language: "We've got over 200 policies and they all need reviewing every year. Half of them are out of date because I just don't have the time. When CQC turn up and ask for a specific policy, I'm praying it's been updated. And even when I do update one, getting every member of staff to actually read it is another battle entirely."
outcome_statement: Policies drafted in minutes instead of hours, automatically flagged when regulations change, and every staff member's acknowledgement tracked digitally — so you're always confident your policy suite is current and compliant.
regulatory_alignment: Maps to CQC Quality Statement: 'Governance, management and sustainability' under the Well-led key question — providers must demonstrate effective governance systems with up-to-date policies that reflect current legislation and best practice. Also aligns with 'Shared direction and culture' (Well-led) — policies set the standard for how care is delivered. CQC Regulation 17 (Good governance) requires providers to maintain securely an accurate, complete and contemporaneous record in respect of each service user. Multiple CQC regulations reference specific policy requirements (e.g., Regulation 13 on safeguarding policy, Regulation 12 on medication policy).
existing_tech_gap: QCS is the market leader in UK care policy content, providing regularly updated template policies and procedures, but still requires manual local customisation and does not use AI for drafting or gap analysis. PolicyPros, W&P Training, SRG, and e-Care Hub offer similar template libraries. Care management platforms (Nourish, PCS) do not include policy management functionality. No mainstream UK care sector platform uses AI to draft bespoke policies, automatically detect regulatory changes, or intelligently review existing policies against current standards. AI adds unique value through generative drafting, regulatory change monitoring, and automated compliance gap analysis across the entire policy suite.
implementation_complexity: Plug-and-play for AI-assisted policy drafting and review tools — these can operate independently of care planning systems. Requires integration for linking policy management to staff training records (to track who has read what) and to the care home's existing document management system. Process redesign is minimal for homes already using digital policy libraries, but significant for homes still managing policies in paper folders or unstructured Word documents.
proof_of_concept_availability: Partial. ReflowAI offers AI-powered compliance automation for UK care homes including documentation generation, reporting 40–60 admin hours saved per month. QCS provides continuously updated policy templates used by thousands of UK care providers but without AI capabilities. General-purpose AI writing tools (ChatGPT, Claude) can generate policy drafts but lack care-sector-specific regulatory knowledge and CQC alignment. No turnkey AI product exists that combines policy drafting, regulatory change monitoring, version control, and staff acknowledgement tracking specifically for UK care homes.
